source: BEP Research — public pricing pages, earnings calls (Q4 FY2025/Q3 FY2026), SEC filings, press releases
methodology: Token Cost Waterfall maps the margin stack from GPU silicon to end-customer. Three layers: (1) Model Provider — what it costs to produce and sell tokens, (2) Enterprise Platform — what SaaS companies pay model providers and charge customers, (3) End Customer — effective cost per AI interaction. Confidence levels: HIGH = public pricing page, MEDIUM = earnings call disclosure + estimate, LOW = analyst inference.

platforms.name: Agentforce
platforms.modelProviderNote: Multi-model routing. xGen for dev tasks, GPT-4o default, Claude via Bedrock. BYOLLM supported.

customerPricing.altPricing: $0.10/action via Flex Credits (May 2025). Also per-user at $125-650/user/month.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: ~10K tokens/conversation. Blended $5/M (volume discount from published $10/M). xGen routing reduces cost on simpler queries.

margins.notes: 3.2 trillion tokens processed. $1.8B ARR (Agentforce + Data Cloud), 114% YoY growth. 9,500+ paid deals. Q3 FY26: 35.5% operating margin.

platforms.name: AI Agents
platforms.modelProvider: OpenAI GPT-4o + Anthropic Claude + Google + Amazon (multi-LLM failover)
platforms.modelProviderNote: Automatic failover between providers for latency. Multi-LLM architecture.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: 2-5K tokens per resolution. Multi-model routing sends simple queries to cheaper models. Only charged on full resolution (no human handoff).

margins.notes: Private company (Hellman & Friedman, $10.2B). Dynamic pricing plan allows remixing seats vs resolutions. Outcome-based pricing = pure margin play.

platforms.dataGap: Private company — no revenue disclosure post-2022 privatization.

platforms.name: Fin AI Agent
platforms.modelProviderNote: Switched from OpenAI to Anthropic Claude for Fin 2 (Oct 2024). Now shipping proprietary Fin Apex 1.0 that beats GPT-5.4 and Claude Sonnet 4.6 on CS benchmarks (73.1% resolution rate). Apex included at no extra cost.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Mix of Claude Haiku ($5/M) for simple queries and Apex 1.0 (internal, near-zero marginal cost) for complex. Blended ~$3/M effective.

margins.notes: Proprietary Apex 1.0 model is a margin expansion play — reduces dependence on external providers. Anthropic itself is a Fin customer. Private company.

platforms.dataGap: Private company — no revenue or margin disclosure.

platforms.name: Now Assist
platforms.modelProviderNote: Claude as default for Build Agent (Jan 2026 Anthropic partnership). Open ecosystem — customers can choose LLMs. Moving toward consumption-based token pricing.

customerPricing.altPricing: $50/mo basic AI, $100+/mo full suite. For 500 fulfillers: $300-600K/year add-on.
customerPricing.source: Analyst estimates, partner pricing guides. Not publicly listed.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: 10 AI interactions/day × 6K tokens × $8/M blended (mix of Claude + proprietary routing). Heavy model routing to cheaper models for classification/triage.

margins.notes: Now Assist ACV surpassed $600M (Q4 FY2025), tracking to $1B by year-end 2026. McDermott bought $23M personal shares. 31% non-GAAP operating margin, 35% FCF margin. Moving to consumption-based pricing.

platforms.dataGap: Per-user pricing not publicly listed. ACV disclosed but per-seat breakdown estimated from partner guides.

platforms.name: M365 Copilot
platforms.modelProviderNote: Microsoft owns 49% of OpenAI. Exclusive cloud provider. Effectively captive supply.

customerPricing.altPricing: Business/SMB: $21/user/month. Copilot Pro (individual): $20/month. Base M365 E3 rising to $39/user/month July 2026.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: 12 interactions/day × 4K tokens × $5/M effective (massive volume discount via 49% ownership). Light users cost less, power users cost more.

margins.notes: Microsoft effectively pays itself for inference via OpenAI relationship. M365 base price increase ($36→$39, July 2026) absorbs AI cost. Azure AI revenue growing >100% YoY.

platforms.name: GitHub Copilot
platforms.modelProvider: OpenAI GPT-4o + Anthropic Claude Opus 4 + Google Gemini (Pro+ tier)
platforms.modelProviderNote: Multi-model in Pro+ tier. 300-1500 'premium requests' per month depending on plan.

customerPricing.altPricing: Pro: $10/mo (300 requests). Pro+: $39/mo (1500 requests). Enterprise: $39/user/mo (1000 requests).

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Heavy users: 25 completions/day × 6K tokens × $6/M blended. Light users (~8/day) are profitable. Request caps on premium models limit exposure.

margins.notes: GitHub disclosed Copilot approaching profitability in aggregate (late 2025). Request caps introduced to control cost. Heavy users subsidized by light users. Strategic land-grab for developer ecosystem.

platforms.dataGap: Actual per-user token consumption varies 10x between light and heavy users. Aggregate margin disclosed as 'approaching profitability.'

platforms.name: Joule AI
platforms.modelProviderNote: Joule Studio for custom agents. Multi-provider.

customerPricing.altPricing: Joule Base is free with SAP Cloud. Premium: min 100 units/year (EUR 700/year). 1-8 AI Units per user/month depending on module.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: AI Unit → token mapping not disclosed. Opaque pricing unit makes margin analysis unreliable.

margins.notes: Cloud gross margin ~73%. AI Unit pricing is intentionally opaque — obscures the token-level economics. SAP Cloud revenue EUR 4.7B/quarter, 27% YoY growth.

platforms.dataGap: AI Unit to token conversion unknown. Cannot compute per-token margin.

platforms.name: Workspace + Gemini
platforms.modelProviderNote: Gemini folded into base Workspace plans. No separate AI add-on (discontinued early 2025). Google controls full stack: TPU → model → API → platform.

customerPricing.altPricing: Workspace plans $14-25/user/month total. Former Gemini add-on was $20-30/user/month before being folded in.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: 8 interactions/day × 3K tokens × $2/M (internal TPU cost, not API price). Google's vertical integration = lowest production cost.

margins.notes: Vertically integrated: TPU → Gemini → Workspace. Lowest cost producer. Folding AI into base subscription is a penetration strategy — monetizes through retention, not AI markup. Cloud operating margin 10-12%.

platforms.dataGap: AI cost portion of Workspace increase is estimated.

token_to_api.description: Token production cost → API sell price

compression_points: Model provider margins under pressure as DeepSeek and open-source drive LLMflation below 25
Per-resolution platforms (Zendesk, Intercom, Salesforce) insulated from token deflation — they sell outcomes, not tokens
Per-user platforms (Microsoft, ServiceNow) exposed to power-user cost risk
Proprietary model development (Intercom Apex, Salesforce xGen) is the margin expansion play — cuts out external model provider entirely
arbitrage_opportunities: Salesforce charges $2/conversation against $0.05 model cost — 40x markup. Any company building a competing CRM agent can undercut massively.
GitHub Copilot at $19/user/month is loss-making for power users. Cap introduction signals Microsoft is finding the profitability boundary.
ServiceNow moving to consumption-based pricing — signals confidence that per-token margins are positive and they want to capture more usage upside.
Google folding Gemini into base Workspace — accepting lower margin per user to block competitors. Counter-positioned to Salesforce's high-margin per-conversation model.
waterfall_summary.key_finding: Margin concentrates at the distribution layer, not the production layer. Per-resolution platforms (Zendesk 99%, Intercom 98%, Salesforce 97%) capture the highest margins because they sell outcomes, not tokens. Per-user platforms (Microsoft 76%, ServiceNow 81%) face power-user risk. The investable insight: the enterprise platform layer is a 40-100x markup on model provider cost. The platform is the moat, not the model.
